title: How To Use Obsidian: The MOST USEFUL Feature You're Not Using Right
summary: Tutorial on Obsidian attachment management: default location settings, subfolder-per-project organization, and the key insight of using metadata-rich notes to make attachments findable by context rather than filename. Low direct relevance to SoNash — Obsidian-specific UI features, not transferable patterns.
creator_view: The core insight — making files findable by the thoughts around them rather than their filenames — is valid but Obsidian-specific. Our .research/ analysis pipeline already solves this with structured metadata (analysis.json), tags, and FTS via SQLite. The video is a basic Obsidian tutorial, not a knowledge system design reference.

candidates.name: Context-over-filename retrieval principle
candidates.description: Make files findable by surrounding metadata and linked context rather than filename or folder path.

candidates.name: Subfolder-per-project attachment pattern
candidates.description: Obsidian setting: auto-create attachments/ subfolder per project folder. Keeps assets co-located with notes.
